description: Wave 3 — Testing + Performance + Tooling + Style Guide + I18N

findings.title: Duplicate values-en/strings.xml violates English-only policy
findings.problem: Both `values/strings.xml` (5444 bytes) and `values-en/strings.xml` (81427 bytes) exist. CLAUDE.md mandates a single source: English-only `values/strings.xml`, with Gemini auto-translating 15+ languages (incl. German) on Play Console upload. The duplicate `values-en/` directory was supposed to be removed and would shadow the auto-translation pipeline.
findings.proposed_fix: Delete `app/src/main/res/values-en/strings.xml`. Verify all string keys present in `values-en/` are also present in `values/strings.xml`; merge any unique keys. Confirm Gemini still produces English output (it is the source — should be unchanged). Add a CI check to fail builds if any `values-en/` or `values-de/` directory is reintroduced.
acceptance_criteria: CI check rejects re-introducing values-en/ or values-de/ directories
Play Console upload still produces 15+ translations

findings.title: Repository tests mock DAOs — violates CLAUDE.md "don't mock the database" rule
findings.problem: All repository tests use `mockk(relaxed = true)` for Room DAOs (CachedDocumentDao, CachedTagDao, PendingChangeDao, …). CLAUDE.md is explicit: integration tests must hit a real database, not mocks (rooted in a past incident where mocked tests passed but a prod migration broke). Current setup defeats integration coverage and lets DAO/migration bugs slip.
findings.proposed_fix: Migrate repository tests to use an in-memory Room database via `Room.inMemoryDatabaseBuilder(...)` + the real DAO. Keep only network/API mocks. Tag pure unit tests `@SmallTest` (mocked) and integration tests `@LargeTest` (real DB) so intent is explicit. Update CLAUDE.md if the boundary needs clarification.
acceptance_criteria: Repository tests run against in-memory Room DB
DAO mocks removed from existing tests

findings.title: ScanViewModel, AppLockManager, TokenManager have zero unit tests
findings.problem: Three high-risk classes lack any tests: ScanViewModel (908 LOC, scan flow), AppLockManager (722 LOC, biometric/PIN security), TokenManager (734 LOC, auth token + Keystore recovery). Comparison: HomeViewModel + UploadViewModel both have tests. Bugs in security/auth paths are most likely to ship undetected.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `ScanViewModelTest`, `AppLockManagerTest`, `TokenManagerTest`. Use `runTest {}` + `MainDispatcherRule` for VMs; mock biometric APIs for AppLockManager; use real DataStore (in-memory file) for TokenManager + simulate AEADBadTagException recovery (commit 30305c0). Aim ≥ 80% line coverage per file.
acceptance_criteria: Three new test files exist + run in CI
AppLockManager test covers lockout, biometric, dual-SavedStateHandle paths
TokenManager test covers Keystore corruption + recovery

findings.title: SyncWorker, TrashDeleteWorker, WidgetUpdateWorker lack tests
findings.problem: Only UploadWorker has a test file. The three other workers run critical background flows (sync queue, trash retention, widget refresh) but have no coverage for retry / failure / scheduling. Regressions surface only in production.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `SyncWorkerTest`, `TrashDeleteWorkerTest`, `WidgetUpdateWorkerTest` using `TestWorkerBuilder` or manual stubbing. Mock repository + network monitor; cover Result.success / retry / failure paths. Mirror existing UploadWorker test layout.
acceptance_criteria: Each covers success / retry / failure / network-timeout

findings.title: AuthRepository uses !! on potentially-null error variables — NPE risk on login
findings.problem: Five `!!` usages on `httpsError` / `httpError` inside when-branches. Variables are assigned only when an exception is caught; if neither call throws but a logical failure occurs, the variables stay null and the `!!` crashes the app at login time — a critical user path.
findings.proposed_fix: Initialize errors with non-null fallback (`Exception("Unknown HTTP error")`) OR replace `!!` with elvis: `httpsError ?: someFallback`. Ensure each when-branch handles the null case explicitly. Add unit tests for all branches including the both-succeed-but-validation-fails scenario.
acceptance_criteria: No `!!` operators in AuthRepository error handling

findings.title: MainActivity.onCreate runBlocks on tokenManager.token.first() — startup-blocking
findings.problem: `runBlocking { tokenManager.token.first() }` blocks the UI thread during Activity creation to decide initial navigation route. The comment claims this avoids race conditions on recreation, but it directly increases Time-to-Interactive and is an Android anti-pattern.
findings.proposed_fix: Set the start destination to a Splash/Loading composable. Inside Compose use `LaunchedEffect(Unit) { tokenManager.token.first() }` to load + then navigate. Or move token resolution to `Application.onCreate()` cached into an `AtomicReference<String?>` (see F-006/F-097) so MainActivity reads it without suspending.
acceptance_criteria: Startup token loaded asynchronously (Splash + LaunchedEffect or Application cache)

findings.title: BillingManager owns custom CoroutineScope without lifecycle cleanup
findings.problem: `private val scope = CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.Main)` lives for the singleton's lifetime with no `cancel()` exposed. Audited similar issues in `ServerHealthMonitor` (`pollingJob`) and `NetworkMonitor`. Long-lived scopes accumulate work + leak when the manager is recreated or the app process is recycled.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `fun cancel()` (or override `Closeable.close()`) calling `scope.cancel()`. Tie to the consuming Activity/ViewModel lifecycle via Hilt or expose to `PaperlessApplication.onTerminate`. Long-term: prefer using `@ApplicationScope` injected `CoroutineScope` from a Hilt module so cleanup is centralized.
acceptance_criteria: BillingManager exposes cancel() and is wired to lifecycle
Leak canary (or test) confirms no leaked job after teardown

findings.title: AiAnalysisIntegrationTest uses 6 relaxed MockK mocks — false integration confidence
findings.problem: Test name says "Integration" but the setup creates 6+ `mockk(relaxed = true)` collaborators (Context, tagRepository, documentTypeRepository, suggestionsService, etc.). Relaxed mocks return defaults silently for unspecified interactions; they hide missing behavior and mask integration bugs.
findings.proposed_fix: Either rename to `AiAnalysisServiceTest` and accept unit-test mocking, OR replace relaxed mocks with strict mocks + explicit `coEvery { … } returns …`, OR wire real fakes (in-memory repositories). Document intent in the class kdoc.
acceptance_criteria: Mocks either strict or replaced with fakes / real instances

findings.title: Six untracked TODOs in production code
findings.problem: Six TODOs without GitHub issue references: Get subscriptionStatus from BillingManager, isCreating state on labels VM, tag-creation dialog in step-by-step metadata, DatePicker dialog in UploadComponents, OCR text extraction, premium implementation gate. Without tracking these become invisible tech debt.
findings.proposed_fix: Convert each TODO into a GitHub issue (label `tech-debt`); replace the TODO comment with an issue number reference (`// TODO(#NNN): …`). Add a `Known Debt` section in `docs/KNOWN_ISSUES.md` linking each.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 TODOs reference an open GitHub issue number
CI lints fail when TODO without issue ref is added

findings.title: ProGuard rules over-keep iText classes — APK size + obfuscation cost
findings.problem: `-keep class com.itextpdf.** { *; }` keeps every iText class fully unobfuscated. The companion `-keep abstract class com.itextpdf.** { *; }` is redundant. Net effect: larger DEX, slower R8 pass.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace blanket keeps with targeted `-keepclassmembers` rules for the entry points actually invoked from the app (likely PdfDocument, PdfWriter, ImageDataFactory). Run R8 with `-printseeds` / `-printusage` to identify the real surface area. Measure APK size before/after.
acceptance_criteria: iText keep rules narrowed to required entry points
R8 build succeeds without iText runtime crashes

findings.title: ViewModel tests don't use Turbine — Flow/StateFlow assertions implicit
findings.problem: ViewModel tests rely on `runTest { … advanceUntilIdle() }` then assert the latest `uiState.value`. Intermediate emissions are not captured, so a Flow that emits in the wrong order can still pass.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `app.cash.turbine` to test dependencies. Convert StateFlow assertions to `viewModel.uiState.test { assertThat(awaitItem()).isEqualTo(...) }`. Document the pattern in `docs/TECHNICAL.md` test section.
acceptance_criteria: All ViewModel tests use Turbine for Flow assertions

findings.title: VERIFY git hooks are actually committed (CLAUDE.md vs. inspection drift)
findings.problem: CLAUDE.md (Lokale CI-Checks) describes a pre-push hook that auto-rebases and runs the full release CI, plus a pre-commit hook for syntax checks. A subagent inspecting the repo claimed only the standard sample hooks exist. Either (a) the hooks live under `.git/hooks/` (not in the repo, so each clone re-installs them — risky) or (b) they were lost. Worth confirming + making them install-on-clone.
findings.proposed_fix: Move the hook scripts into a versioned `.githooks/` directory at the repo root. Configure `git config core.hooksPath .githooks` in a one-time bootstrap script (run on `./scripts/setup.sh`). Document the install step in README + CLAUDE.md.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh clone runs hooks after bootstrap
